close_reason: Closed

title: Graphite sync failures cause retry storms
description: gt sync commands failing repeatedly without backoff or circuit breaker. Causes infinite retry loops that contribute to server crashes and agent failures. Seen in GraphiteService logs during high concurrent agent load. Need: 1) Exponential backoff on Graphite command failures, 2) Circuit breaker to disable Graphite temporarily after N failures, 3) Graceful fallback to standard git/gh workflow when Graphite unavailable. Agent work should continue even if Graphite is broken.
notes: Fixed in PR #137 (merged 2026-02-09). Retry logic with exponential backoff (1s→2s→4s) + circuit breaker (3 failures → 60s cooldown) now live. Conflict detection prevents infinite loops.

title: BUG: Worktree build symlink issue
description: npm workspace hoisting means worktrees resolve @automaker/types from main repo (not worktree). Types changes in worktrees fail to compile against main repo types. Code is correct but build fails until merged. Workaround: commit despite build failure, verify on merge.
notes:
## Research Findings (2026-02-06)

Root cause: npm workspace hoisting. node_modules/@automaker/types is a symlink → ../../libs/types. In a worktree at .worktrees/feature-xxx/, this resolves to the MAIN repo's libs/types, not the worktree's copy. So agents modifying types in a worktree build against stale main-repo compiled types.

No npm install happens during worktree creation. No worktree-specific build logic exists.

Recommended fix (Option C - Hybrid): Run npm run build:packages after worktree creation. This rebuilds libs/types/dist/ in the main repo, which is what worktrees resolve to. Lightweight, targeted. Could be added to worktree creation route or init-script-service.

Better long-term: Run npm install in each worktree (creates isolated node_modules). More disk space but full isolation.

Key files: apps/server/src/routes/worktree/routes/create.ts (worktree creation), apps/server/src/services/init-script-service.ts (init scripts)


## Antagonistic Review Findings (2026-02-07)

Original approach (rebuild packages on worktree creation) REJECTED:
- FATAL FLAW: Breaks concurrent worktree safety - Agent A rebuilding main repo dist breaks Agent B's build
- Scope creep: Solving agent behavior problem with infrastructure complexity
- Performance lie: Rebuilds ALL packages on EVERY worktree (30s overhead)
- Wrong diagnosis: Issue is agent prompt gap, not worktree architecture

## Revised Approach (APPROVED)

Fix agent prompts to teach monorepo build order:
- Backend agents: Run npm run build:packages when modifying libs/
- Frontend agents: Document limitation (no Bash), delegate to backend
- Zero infrastructure changes
- Zero overhead for features that don't modify shared packages
- Maintains worktree isolation and security boundaries

PRD: /private/tmp/.../worktree-build-fix-prd-revised.md

Ready for ProjM handoff.

Need to either: (1) Fix plugin MCP server registration, (2) Manually configure ~/.mcp.json, or (3) Investigate why tools arent loading.

title: Dev server crash during self-automation (9 concurrent agents)
description:
Server crashed while 9 agents were running in parallel implementing Beads API + Decision Tracking features.

Trigger: Started auto-mode with maxConcurrency=1, but auto-mode spawned 9 agents concurrently anyway (bug in auto-mode enforcement).

Known issue from memory: 'Server can't handle more than ~2-3 concurrent agents'.

Impact:
- All agent work lost (9 features mid-implementation)
- MCP server unreachable (fetch failed)
- Auto-mode stopped

Root cause: Auto-mode not respecting maxConcurrency limit properly.

Fix needed:
1. Tune maxConcurrency to 2 (proven safe limit)
2. Fix auto-mode to actually enforce the limit
3. Add circuit breaker to prevent overload
notes:
Entered manual agent orchestration mode. Starting agents sequentially with concurrency=1. First agent: Beads REST API Bridge epic (feature-1770710201629-ltyn91r30)
ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED:

TOCTOU race condition in startAutoLoopForProject (auto-mode-service.ts:757-816):

Timeline:
1. Line 772: Check if existingState?.isRunning (no lock)
2. Line 801: Set new state in autoLoopsByProject (no lock)

Race scenario:
- Call 1: Check (line 772) → not running → proceed
- Call 2: Check (line 772) → not running → proceed (race!)
- Call 1: Set state (line 801)
- Call 2: Set state (line 801) → overwrites Call 1
- Call 1: Start loop
- Call 2: Start loop
→ Result: 2 independent loops, each respecting maxConcurrency=1
→ With 9 rapid concurrent calls: 9 loops × 1 concurrent = 9 agents

FIX:
Add mutex/lock or use atomic check-and-set pattern.
Example: Use Map.has() + Map.set() with proper locking.

Evidence:
- Multiple entry points call startAutoLoopForProject (EM agent, GOAP, HTTP routes, restart logic)
- All check line 772-773 without synchronization
- Server likely received multiple rapid requests during initialization/restart
CRASH EVENT 2026-02-10 ~08:52 UTC:
Server crashed while 1 agent was running (race condition bugfix).
Agent was running tests (npm run test:server).
MCP health check failed with 'fetch failed'.

Different from original crash (9 concurrent agents).
Possible causes:
1. Test suite resource exhaustion
2. Agent tooluse triggering server issue
3. Unrelated server stability problem

Impact:
- Race condition fix agent work potentially lost
- MCP tools unavailable
- Need server restart

Waiting for user to restart server per operational constraints.
Using exponential backoff to monitor recovery.
SERVER RECOVERED (08:53 UTC):
Server back online after ~1min downtime.
Race condition agent work preserved in worktree (116 lines changed).
Agent restarted to complete PR creation.

Recovery successful via exponential backoff (attempt 2/5, 60s wait).
SECOND CRASH (08:57 UTC):
Server crashed again ~4min after recovery.
Agent was running (race condition fix continuation).
This is 2nd crash in <10 minutes.

Pattern emerging:
- Crash 1: Agent running tests (npm run test:server)
- Crash 2: Agent restarted, likely running tests again

Hypothesis: Test suite execution may be triggering crash
(memory exhaustion, resource leak, async operation timeout)

Action: Continuing exponential backoff.
Consider: Investigate test suite resource usage separately.
close_reason: Root causes addressed across multiple PRs: PR #162 (concurrency tracking fix), PR #195 (configurable heap thresholds), PR #244 (tsx watch zombie fix), PR #272 (auto-mode idle loop fix), PR #275 (server logs for post-mortem). Race condition documented. Max safe concurrency = 2-3 agents on dev, 6-10 on staging.

title: IMPROVE: Discord bot token auto-config
description: Discord MCP isn't usable from the plugin due to env var interpolation bug. Need either: (1) fix plugin env var interpolation, or (2) alternative token injection path. This blocks Ava from sending Discord messages autonomously.
notes:
## Analysis (2026-02-07)

Current state:
- Discord MCP tools exist via Automaker plugin (mcp__plugin_automaker_discord__*)
- Tools fail with 'Discord client not logged in' error
- ROOT CAUSE: Plugin env var interpolation bug (automaker-c9u)

Working workaround:
- Use Discord REST API directly with curl and $DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN
- Successfully tested sending messages to #ava-josh channel
- Sufficient for current autonomous operations

Conclusion:
- No standalone Discord MCP package exists (@discordjs/mcp = 404)
- Can't add to .mcp.json as separate server
- Fixing this requires fixing automaker-c9u (plugin env var interpolation)
- REST API workaround is acceptable until then

Recommendation: DEFER until automaker-c9u is addressed. REST API works fine.
Closed as WONTFIX - REST API workaround is sufficient. Underlying issue tracked in automaker-c9u.

title: Explore hooks + programmatic CLI for Ava quick wins
description:
Josh suggested using Claude Code hooks and programmatic CLI (claude -p) to create listeners/automation that keep Ava running while waiting for full pipeline tools. Quick wins to get started with self-automation.

Research:
- Hooks guide: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide
- Programmatic usage: https://code.claude.com/docs/llms.txt
- Agent SDK: TypeScript/Python packages

Potential applications:
- Hook on feature:created → auto-start agent
- Hook on PR:merged → close Beads task
- Programmatic CLI for scheduled checks
- Event listeners for board monitoring
notes:
RESEARCH COMPLETE - Key Findings:

**HOOKS (Event-driven automation):**
Available events for Ava:
- SessionStart: inject context when resuming
- PostToolUse: trigger actions after tool calls
- Stop: verify completion before allowing Claude to stop  
- Notification: desktop alerts when attention needed
- TaskCompleted: automation when tasks finish

Hook types:
- command: run shell script (10min timeout default)
- prompt: ask Claude Haiku for decision (single-turn)
- agent: spawn subagent with tools (60s timeout, 50 turns)

Hook scope:
- ~/.claude/settings.json (global, all projects)
- .claude/settings.json (project-specific, shareable)
- skill/agent frontmatter (active while skill runs)

**PROGRAMMATIC CLI (Non-interactive automation):**
Key flags:
- claude -p "prompt" (non-interactive mode)
- --allowedTools "Tool1,Tool2" (auto-approve)
- --continue (resume last conversation)
- --resume session_id (resume specific conversation)
- --output-format json (structured responses)
- --append-system-prompt (add instructions)

**AVA AUTOMATION OPPORTUNITIES:**

1. AUTO-START AGENTS ON FEATURE CREATION
Hook: PostToolUse matcher "mcp__.*__create_feature"
Action: Parse featureId from JSON, call start_agent MCP tool
Impact: Zero-delay agent startup

2. PR MERGE → CLOSE BEADS TASK
Script: gh pr list --state merged + bd close [matching tasks]
Trigger: Cron job every 5min or PostToolUse on gh pr merge
Impact: Automatic cleanup, no manual tracking

3. CONTINUOUS HEALTH MONITORING
Script: claude -p "check board status, start agents if needed" --continue
Trigger: Cron every 10min, use --resume to maintain context
Impact: Never idle, always working

4. DISCORD NOTIFICATIONS
Hook: PostToolUse on agent completion → curl Discord webhook
Include: Feature title, PR link, duration
Impact: Josh gets real-time updates

5. STOP HOOK VERIFICATION  
Hook: Stop with agent-based verification
Prompt: "Check if all features are done, tests pass, PRs created"
Impact: Prevents premature sign-off, ensures thoroughness

**RECOMMENDED FIRST IMPLEMENTATION:**
PostToolUse hook for auto-starting agents when features are created.

Example:
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PostToolUse": [{
      "matcher": "mcp__.*__create_feature",
      "hooks": [{
        "type": "command",
        "command": "jq -r '.result.feature.id' | xargs -I {} claude -p \"Start agent for feature {}\" --allowedTools mcp__.*__start_agent"
      }]
    }]
  }
}
```

NEXT STEPS:
1. Create feature for hook implementation
2. Start with auto-agent-start hook (highest ROI)
3. Add PR merge automation
4. Add continuous health check loop
5. Add Discord notifications last
close_reason: Research complete. Documented 5 automation opportunities: auto-start agents, PR merge cleanup, health monitoring, Discord notifications, and Stop hook verification. Recommended starting with PostToolUse hook for auto-agent-start (highest ROI).

title: Explore code-based orchestration for complex workflows
description:
Research complete. RECOMMENDATION: Evaluate for specific high-value use cases.

FINDINGS:
- 37% token reduction (43.5K → 27.3K tokens)
- Eliminates N-1 inference passes for N-tool workflows
- 28.5% vs 25.6% accuracy on retrieval tasks

HOW IT WORKS:
1. Mark tools with allowed_callers config
2. Claude writes Python orchestration code
3. Tools execute in sandbox, results stay out of context
4. Only final output returns to Claude

BEST USE CASES:
- Large dataset → summary (20+ parallel queries)
- Multi-step workflows (3+ dependent ops)
- Filter/transform before context

AUTOMAKER APPLICATIONS:
1. Auto-mode: Query 50+ features → filter → return top 5
2. ProjM: Fetch PRD + files → decompose → phases
3. Board reconciliation: Query all → return mismatches only

NEXT: Create PRD for highest-value application (likely auto-mode or ProjM)

Sources: Anthropic advanced tool use, ikangai.com, DEV community

STATUS: Ready for prioritization against other improvements.

title: BUG: Agent turn limits on medium complexity
description: Agents frequently run out of turns on medium complexity features. Current mitigation: bump to large/architectural and include 'Previous Agent Progress' in descriptions. Need a systemic fix - either increase default turns or better complexity estimation.
notes:
## Research Findings (2026-02-06)

All features get 1000 turns (MAX_TURNS.maximum in sdk-options.ts) regardless of complexity. Complexity only maps to MODEL selection, not turn limits.

Root cause: 1000 turns can be tight for Sonnet on complex multi-file features. Each tool invocation (Read, Edit, Bash, Glob) counts as a turn. No turn-limit-specific escalation exists — error_max_turns is treated as generic failure.

Current escalation: after 2+ failures → model escalates to Opus. Turn limit stays at 1000.

Proposed fixes (from most to least impactful):
1. Detect error_max_turns specifically, retry with more turns
2. Map complexity → turn limits (small:200, medium:500, large:1000, arch:1500)
3. Add maxTurns field to Feature type for manual override
4. Increase default from 1000 to 1500 for auto-mode

Key files: apps/server/src/lib/sdk-options.ts (MAX_TURNS), apps/server/src/services/auto-mode-service.ts (getModelForFeature), apps/server/src/providers/simple-query-service.ts (error_max_turns handling)
close_reason: Fixed in PR #119 - added max_turns detection and retry with exponential backoff (MAX_MAX_TURNS_RETRIES=3). Also added per-project failure tracking.

title: IMPROVE: Design agile rituals for agentic teams
description: Need to define cadence for standups, retros, sprint planning that makes sense for AI agents. Not 1:1 copy of human agile - what actually works for async AI collaboration? When do agents report? How do we track velocity? What's the right granularity for sprints?
close_reason: Defer - design work for future. Need to define agile rituals for AI teams when we have larger teams running.

title: BUG: Plugin env var interpolation broken
description: Not a bug - Claude Code intentionally doesn't interpolate env vars in plugin.json (security). Workaround: .mcp.json at repo root, wrapper script, or Infisical. Low priority - .mcp.json workaround works fine.
notes:
## Resolution (2026-02-07)

This is NOT a bug - it's intentional Claude Code behavior (security design).

Claude Code does not interpolate env vars in plugin.json to prevent plugins from accessing arbitrary environment variables. This is a security boundary.

Working workarounds:
1. .mcp.json at repo root (CURRENT APPROACH) - Works perfectly
2. Wrapper scripts - More complex, unnecessary
3. Infisical/secrets management - Overkill for local dev

Current state:
- Linear MCP: Configured in .mcp.json with ${LINEAR_API_KEY} interpolation ✓
- Automaker staging: Configured in .mcp.json with explicit API key ✓
- Discord: REST API workaround using $DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN ✓

No action needed. Close as WONTFIX.

title: [Ava Alert] Board attention needed
description:
**ALERT: critical Empty Board State**

The Automaker development board is reporting **zero features** across all statuses. This is highly abnormal given the MEMORY.md shows the board should have 137 features (3 backlog, 126 done).

**Why this needs attention:**
1. This could indicate a data integrity issue with the feature storage system
2. The MCP connection to the Automaker server may be failing
3. The board query might be erroring silently
4. Given the Feb 10 feature data loss incident documented in MEMORY.md, this pattern is familiar

**Recommended immediate actions:**
1. Check if the dev server is running and healthy
2. Verify `.automaker/features/` directory contains feature.json files
3. Check server logs for errors in feature loading
4. Test MCP tool connectivity with a simple board query
5. If data is missing, restore from git history (feature.json files are now git-tracked per PR #164)

This requires immediate investigation before any other work proceeds.**ALERT: critical Empty Board State**

title: Research claude-flow framework for agent orchestration
description:
Research complete. RECOMMENDATION: Learn from patterns, don't adopt wholesale.

FINDINGS:
- 60+ specialized agents, 5 consensus algorithms, distributed swarm intelligence
- 30-50% token reduction through intelligent routing
- Sub-50ms adaptation, 150x-12,500x faster retrieval (HNSW vectors)
- Byzantine fault tolerance, anti-drift mechanisms

KEY LEARNINGS FOR AUTOMAKER:
1. Anti-drift patterns (hierarchical topology, checkpoints, role clarity)
2. Distributed consensus for mission-critical decisions
3. Q-Learning routing (task→agent assignment based on success)
4. HNSW vector search for code relationships
5. Agent Booster (WASM for <1ms transforms vs LLM calls)

WHY NOT FULL ADOPTION:
- Complexity overkill (we don't need 60+ agents or 5 consensus algos)
- We have simpler needs: 5 authority agents, sequential workflows mostly
- MIT license ✅ but integration cost high

ACTIONABLE TAKEAWAYS:
1. Implement anti-drift for ProjM/EM (role clarity, checkpoints)
2. Add success-based routing (track which model complexity works best)
3. Evaluate HNSW for code search vs current grep/glob
4. Consider WASM for simple transforms (file parsing, JSON manipulation)

NEXT: Create improvement tasks for highest-ROI patterns (anti-drift, success routing)

Source: github.com/ruvnet/claude-flow

STATUS: Learnings extracted, full framework adoption not recommended.

title: Implement auto-start agent hook for feature creation
description:
Implement PostToolUse hook that automatically starts an agent when a feature is created via MCP tools.

From research (automaker-7zu):
Hook triggers when mcp__.*__create_feature tool is used.
Extracts featureId from result JSON.
Calls start_agent MCP tool automatically.

Benefits:
- Zero-delay agent startup
- No manual agent start needed
- Ava can create features and they auto-execute

Implementation:
1. Add PostToolUse hook to plugin hooks configuration
2. Matcher: "mcp__.*__create_feature"
3. Command: Parse JSON → extract feature.id → call start_agent
4. Test with feature creation
5. Verify agent starts automatically

Location: packages/mcp-server/plugins/automaker/hooks/hooks.json

Acceptance:
- Create feature via MCP → agent starts automatically
- Works in both interactive and programmatic mode
- No regression in existing feature creation
notes:
VALIDATION: Ava's workflow is PRD → ProjM → features. Ava doesn't create features directly via MCP. This hook would only help if creating one-off features, which isn't the pattern. May be premature. Need to validate use case before implementing.
ELEVATED TO P0 per Josh directive. Auto-start hook is core automation for continuous operation. This enables self-improvement loops without manual intervention.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #229. PostToolUse hook auto-starts agent on create_feature. Guards for epics, human-assigned, and features with deps.

title: Investigate and prevent phantom git worktree registrations
description:
## Problem

During auto-mode troubleshooting (2026-02-09), discovered phantom git worktree registrations blocking feature selection. Worktree directories were deleted but git still listed them in `git worktree list`, causing auto-mode to skip features thinking they were already in use.

## What Happened

1. Agent runs were interrupted (server crash during emergency concurrency fix)
2. Worktree directories were manually removed with `rm -rf`
3. Git metadata still had worktree registrations
4. Auto-mode filtered out features: "❌ Filtering out feature... (branchName: X has worktree)"
5. Required manual `git worktree remove --force` for each phantom registration

## Investigation Needed

1. **Root cause**: Why do worktree directories get orphaned when agents/server crash?
2. **Detection**: How can we detect phantom registrations automatically?
3. **Auto-recovery**: Should we auto-prune on server startup or periodically?

## Proposed Solutions

- Add `git worktree prune` to server startup routine
- Periodic background task to detect and clean phantom worktrees
- Improve agent cleanup to always use `git worktree remove` instead of directory deletion
- Add health check endpoint that verifies worktree integrity

## Priority

Medium - Doesn't block work but causes confusing auto-mode failures after crashes.

## Files to Investigate

- apps/server/src/services/auto-mode-service.ts (worktree creation/cleanup)
- apps/server/src/lib/worktree-metadata.ts (worktree management)
- Agent cleanup/error handling code
notes:
PRD complete: Worktree Recovery System. 3-phase approach: (1) Startup prune removes phantom registrations, (2) Periodic health check every 6h auto-prunes, (3) Health API endpoint for monitoring. Safe operations (only removes stale metadata). 7-10 hours estimate. Ready for submission.
PRD submitted to ProjM (feature ID pending). ProjM will decompose into tasks.

title: PRD Intake Bridge - API endpoint for Ava to submit PRDs to ProjM
description:
## SPARC PRD: CoS PRD Intake Bridge (Rev 2 - Post Antagonistic Review)

### Reviewer Feedback Incorporated
4 critical issues found and resolved:
1. ProjM agent initialization guard — endpoint must init all agents before emitting events
2. SPARC PRD struct not consumed by ProjM — simplify to description (markdown), milestones, complexity
3. Event type must be added to EventType union — TypeScript build break otherwise
4. Project slug collisions — append timestamp suffix for uniqueness

### Situation
Ava (CoS) researches problems and creates PRDs but has no mechanism to hand them to ProjM for execution. ProjM listens for authority:pm-review-approved events but those only come from the PM agent. Dead end.

### Problem
No API endpoint exists for CoS to submit approved PRDs to ProjM. The existing inject-idea endpoint routes through PM for research, but Ava has ALREADY done the research. She needs to skip PM and feed ProjM directly.

### Approach
1. Add cos:prd-submitted to EventType union in libs/types/src/event.ts
2. Create POST /api/cos/submit-prd route (new route group apps/server/src/routes/cos/)
3. Handler accepts: projectPath, title, description (full markdown PRD), complexity, milestones [{title, description}]
4. Handler initializes all authority agents for projectPath (CRITICAL - ProjM has initializedProjects guard)
5. Handler creates feature: workItemState=approved, isEpic=true, category=CoS Projects, description=PRD markdown
6. Handler emits authority:pm-review-approved with payload ProjM expects: {projectPath, featureId, complexity, milestones}
7. Also emits cos:prd-submitted for tracking
8. Handler generates slug with timestamp suffix to prevent collisions
9. Add submit_prd MCP tool to packages/mcp-server/src/index.ts
10. Add mcp__automaker__submit_prd to Ava's allowed-tools in ava.md
11. Register route in apps/server/src/index.ts: app.use('/api/cos', createCosRoutes(events, featureLoader, agents))

### Results
- One MCP tool is Ava's single point of contact with the execution layer
- ProjM automatically decomposes PRDs into milestones/phases/board features
- ProjM manages agents, PRs, Discord reporting downstream
- Clean separation maintained: Ava creates PRDs, ProjM manages execution

### Constraints
- Must init all authority agents before emitting (copy inject-idea pattern from authority routes lines 244-247)
- Accept description as markdown string, NOT structured SPARC object (ProjM uses feature.description as project goal)
- Must add cos:prd-submitted to EventType union FIRST (types package, rebuild before server)
- Slug generation must include timestamp suffix: slug-1707123456789
- Route must pass agents object for initialization: createCosRoutes(events, featureLoader, agents)

### Files to Create
- apps/server/src/routes/cos/index.ts (router factory)
- apps/server/src/routes/cos/routes/submit-prd.ts (handler)

### Files to Modify
- libs/types/src/event.ts (add cos:prd-submitted to EventType union)
- apps/server/src/index.ts (import and mount /api/cos routes, pass agents)
- packages/mcp-server/src/index.ts (add submit_prd tool)
- packages/mcp-server/plugins/automaker/commands/ava.md (add mcp__automaker__submit_prd to allowed-tools)

### Milestones
M1: Types + API — Add event type, create route with agent initialization, feature creation, event emission
M2: MCP Tool + Skill — Add submit_prd to MCP server, add to Ava's allowed-tools
M3: Integration Verify — End-to-end test: submit PRD -> ProjM creates project -> features appear on board
notes: PRD reviewed (antagonistic). 4 critical issues found and resolved. Features created on Automaker board: Phase 1 (feature-1770411847535) and Phase 2 (feature-1770411851597). Auto-mode started. Delegated to ProjM pipeline. Monitoring from Beads.

title: Add anti-drift mechanisms to authority agents
description:
Prevent goal divergence in PM/ProjM/EM workflows.

Patterns from claude-flow:
- Role clarity checkpoints (verify agent staying in scope)
- Hierarchical topology (clear authority chain)
- Progress validation (milestones, not just completion)

Target agents: PM, ProjM, EM
Effort: 2-3 days
Impact: MEDIUM-HIGH (reduce agent failures, improve output quality)

From: automaker-e7b research
notes:
## RESEARCH COMPLETE - SPARC PRD DRAFT

### S - Situation
Authority agents (PM, ProjM, EM) operate autonomously with minimal oversight. Current workflow:
- PM: idea → research → PRD generation → approval
- ProjM: approved idea → milestone planning → feature decomposition
- EM: ready features → agent orchestration → PR management

**Drift risks identified:**
1. PM generating PRDs unrelated to original idea
2. ProjM decomposing beyond original scope
3. EM orchestrating work on wrong features
4. No validation that output matches input

### P - Problem
Authority agents can drift from their mandate during long-running operations. Evidence from claude-flow research:
- Multi-turn conversations lose context
- Goal divergence increases with task complexity  
- No checkpoints to verify alignment with original objective

**Impact:** Wasted API calls, incorrect work, scope creep, failed features.

### A - Approach
Implement three anti-drift mechanisms:

**1. Role Clarity Checkpoints (Pre-flight validation)**
Before major state transitions, inject system reminder:
"You are [ROLE]. Your mandate is [SCOPE]. Verify this action aligns with your role."

**2. Progress Validation (Post-action verification)**
After PRD generation/planning, use fast model (haiku) to validate:
- Does PRD address original idea?
- Does milestone plan match PRD scope?
- Are features within complexity bounds?

**3. Hierarchical Topology (Authority chain enforcement)**
Already partially implemented via AuthorityService.submitProposal().
Enhance with scope validation:
- PM can only modify workItemState for ideas/PRDs
- ProjM can only modify milestones/planning
- EM can only orchestrate ready features

**Implementation approach:**
1. Add checkpoint utilities to agent-utils.ts
2. Inject checkpoints into PM/ProjM/EM workflows
3. Add validation step after generation (haiku-based)
4. Log drift detection events for observability

### R - Results
**Success metrics:**
- Agent drift incidents: 0 per week (baseline: unknown, estimated 1-2)
- False positive validations: <5% (acceptable threshold)
- Average response time increase: <500ms per checkpoint

**Deliverables:**
- Checkpoint utilities in agent-utils.ts
- PM agent: pre-PRD checkpoint + post-PRD validation
- ProjM agent: pre-planning checkpoint + post-planning validation
- EM agent: pre-orchestration checkpoint
- Drift detection events emitted
- Unit tests for each checkpoint

### C - Constraints
- **Performance**: Checkpoints must not double latency
- **Cost**: Validation uses haiku only (<$0.01 per validation)
- **False positives**: Must allow legitimate scope expansion with justification
- **Backward compatibility**: Existing features must work unchanged
- **Observability**: All drift detections must be logged/emitted
CANCELLED per Josh directive 2026-02-10. Refocusing on automation and self-improvement for full autonomy. Drift mechanisms deprioritized.

title: Evaluate MCP tool search for dynamic tool loading
description:
Research complete. RECOMMENDATION: Adopt MCP tool search with auto:10 threshold.

FINDINGS:
- We have 49 MCP tools (near/at 10% threshold)
- Expected savings: 40-50% context reduction (85% with 50+ tools)
- Implementation: 3 lines (ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH env var)
- Compatible: Sonnet 4.5/Opus 4.5 ✅, Haiku fallback acceptable

IMPLEMENTATION:
```typescript
const options = {
  env: { ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH: 'auto:10' }
};
```

ROLLOUT:
1. Test with 'true' in dev
2. Measure savings
3. Deploy 'auto:10' to prod
4. Monitor Haiku impact

Sources: Medium/Njenga, Claude SDK docs, Steve Oak, Cyrus, techbuddies.io

NEXT: Create feature for implementation.
close_reason: Already implemented. ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=auto:10 is set in plugin.json line 16. Deployed with plugin v1.0.6.

title: Improve Ava's tool usage patterns
description:
As Ava works, track friction points and flaky tool calls. Document patterns that fail frequently (Discord MCP login timeouts, API key issues, tool search failures) and propose improvements to the /ava skill that make these operations more reliable.

**Examples of friction:**
- Discord MCP requires explicit login with token every session (should cache or auto-retry)
- Automaker staging MCP fails with 403 errors (should fall back to filesystem reads)
- Tool search returns multiple versions of same tool (should intelligently pick the right one)

**Goal:** Make Ava's workflow smoother by identifying patterns Josh can improve from his end (skill updates, tool wrappers, better error handling).

title: Decision store with querying and audit trail
description: Decision Store with audit trail. RESEARCH COMPLETE: (1) Use JSONL + JSON index pattern (matches AuditService in codebase), (2) Append-only immutability prevents overwrites, git-friendly diffs, (3) Schema: id, timestamp, agent, type, decision, rationale, context, tags, relatedIds, status, (4) Endpoints: POST /api/decisions (log), GET /api/decisions/list (query by agent/type/tags/date), GET /api/decisions/:id (retrieve), POST /:id/approve|reject|supersede, (5) Scales to 10k decisions with in-memory filtering, SQLite if >10k. Implementation: DecisionService + routes layer, ~1-2 days.
notes: PRD revised and submitted to ProjM (feature-1770710210160-4ltbysh6o). Pivoted to extending AuditService instead of parallel DecisionService. Avoids dual sources of truth, reuses proven patterns. Estimate 4-5 days.
